other_observers: David Trappett, Diane Trappett; As far as I know I was the original observer of these birds.  Many other birders independently observed the birds as they were found during the Mesa CBC count.
description: Two males and one female were observed. A small finch similar in size to a siskin, but with very distinct red coloring and yellow bill. Males and female both had a red cap, yellow bill and dark face. Males had pink on the breast, and all three had dark streaking on the flanks.Behaviors: The birds were feeding at two thistle feeders amongst a small flock of Pine Siskins, and American Goldfinches. Other birds were also present and feeding from a sunflower feeder including House Finches and House Sparrows. The birds spent 30 to 45 seconds at the feeders and then flushed to nearby trees as a flock, before filtering down one by one back to the feeders and repeating the cycle the whole time we watched.Call: There were many birds at the feeders and we were unable to single out calls from the Redpolls.Plumage: adult
